wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Titchybook Creator project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a micro booklets creation platform that transforms user-uploaded images into printable 8-page micro booklets with professional PDF generation. Document the key features including user authentication, image upload and validation, PDF generation pipeline, submission management, and administrative features. Describe the technology stack including Next.js 16, React 19, Prisma ORM, AWS S3 integration, and pdf-lib. Include the target audience, core benefits, and how the platform solves the problem of creating professional micro booklets. Provide a high-level architecture overview showing the relationship between frontend components, backend APIs, database layer, and cloud storage integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: NextAuth Configuration & Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for NextAuth configuration in Titchybook Creator. Explain the JWT strategy implementation, provider setup with custom credentials provider, and module augmentation for TypeScript types. Document the callback functions for JWT and session management, including token-to-session mapping and user role propagation. Cover the NextAuth configuration object structure, provider credentials definition, and authentication pages setup. Include examples of custom authorize function implementation, password validation with bcryptjs, and user object construction. Address session strategy selection, token expiration handling, and security considerations for JWT implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for Titchybook Creator's Next.js application. Detail the App Router implementation with route groups for authentication, protected routes, and admin functionality. Explain the component hierarchy starting from the root layout through Providers to individual pages. Document the Providers pattern for session management and state sharing across components. Describe the routing strategy using route groups and how they control access permissions. Include the header component architecture and its role in navigation. Explain client-server boundary concepts, server components vs client components, and data fetching patterns. Document the component composition patterns and how different page types (auth, protected, admin) are structured. Address styling architecture with Tailwind CSS and component reusability patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the User model in Titchybook Creator's database. Detail the User entity structure including id (String, @id, @default(cuid())), email (String, @unique), passwordHash (String), name (String?), role (String, @default('USER')), and timestamps (createdAt, updatedAt). Explain the role field with USER and ADMIN values, authentication mechanisms, and user profile management. Document the relationship with Submission model through submissions[]. Include field validation rules, unique constraints, default values, and indexing strategies. Provide examples of user creation, authentication workflows, and role-based access patterns. Address security considerations for password hashing and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Design and Layout
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the PDF template design and layout system. Document the A4 landscape page dimensions (297mm x 210mm) and the coordinate system used for positioning panels. Explain the PANELS configuration object that defines page layout, including x, y coordinates, width, height, and rotation properties for each of the 8 panels. Detail the mmToPoints conversion utility and how it translates millimeter measurements to PDF points. Document the page positioning logic that converts from top-left origin (CSS spec) to bottom-left origin (pdf-lib standard). Include examples of how panels are arranged on the page and how the layout accommodates different image orientations. Address customization options for modifying panel positions, sizes, and spacing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Upload Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the upload interface components in Titchybook Creator. Document the ImageUploader component implementation including drag-and-drop functionality, file input handling, and visual feedback states. Explain the UploadGrid component for managing multiple image uploads and page arrangement. Detail the page labeling system with PAGE_LABEL_DISPLAY constants and how users interact with individual upload slots. Include component props, event handlers, and state management patterns. Document the visual design system with Tailwind CSS classes for drag-over states, loading indicators, and error display. Provide examples of component usage, customization options, and integration patterns with the parent form components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Submission Tracking and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the submission tracking and management system. Document the SubmissionList component implementation showing user's booklet creation history with submission details, dates, and status indicators. Explain the submission CRUD operations including GET requests for listing submissions, POST for creation, and DELETE for removal. Detail the submission data model structure with fields like title, status, creation date, and associated images. Document the integration with the backend API routes for data retrieval and manipulation. Include examples of submission filtering by status, sorting by date, and pagination handling. Address user permissions for accessing and modifying their own submissions only.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the admin dashboard interface in Titchybook Creator. Document the admin dashboard component structure, layout organization, and navigation elements. Explain the dashboard's role in providing administrative controls for content moderation and user management. Detail the interface components including submission lists, user management panels, and administrative controls. Document the dashboard's integration with authentication middleware and role-based access control. Include examples of dashboard layout, component composition, and user interaction patterns. Address responsive design considerations and accessibility features. Provide guidance on customizing dashboard views and adding new administrative widgets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document NextAuth integration endpoints including OAuth flows, session management, and callback handling. Detail login/logout endpoints with JWT token handling and session persistence. Document user registration API with validation rules, password hashing, and role assignment. Include request/response schemas for authentication payloads, error responses, and security headers. Provide examples for social login integration, custom callbacks, and session refresh mechanisms. Document authentication middleware requirements, token expiration handling, and logout procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for layout components in Titchybook Creator. Document the Header component including navigation links, authentication state handling, role-based menu items, and responsive design patterns. Explain the Providers component for session management and NextAuth integration. Detail the root layout structure and page composition patterns. Include usage examples showing component integration, styling options with Tailwind CSS classes, and responsive behavior. Document component props, event handlers, and customization guidelines. Address accessibility considerations, navigation patterns, and user experience best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Variables & Secrets
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for environment variables and secrets management in Titchybook Creator. Document all required environment variables including NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL, DATABASE_URL, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_REGION, AWS_BUCKET_NAME, and NEXT_PUBLIC_AWS_BUCKET_URL. Explain variable naming conventions, data types, and validation requirements. Detail security best practices for storing sensitive credentials, rotation procedures, and local development vs production differences. Include examples of .env file structure, Docker environment configuration, and CI/CD pipeline variable management. Address common configuration errors, debugging techniques, and environment-specific overrides.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Titchybook Creator. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including cloning the repository, installing dependencies, and configuring environment variables. Document the development server setup process with clear commands and expected outputs. Include a first-run guide with browser verification and initial feature exploration. Cover common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide guidance on project structure navigation and key directories users should know about. Include environment variable configuration examples and database setup requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration & Login Flows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for user registration and login functionality. Detail the login form implementation with email/password validation, error handling, and authentication flow. Document the registration form with user input validation, password hashing using bcryptjs, and database user creation. Explain the API endpoints for authentication, request/response schemas, and error responses. Cover form validation patterns, client-side validation, server-side validation, and security measures. Include examples of successful authentication flows, error scenarios, and user feedback mechanisms. Address password security, input sanitization, and protection against common authentication vulnerabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend architecture documentation for Titchybook Creator's server-side implementation. Document the middleware system for route protection and authentication flow. Explain the NextAuth integration with custom callbacks and session management. Detail the API route structure organized by feature areas (auth, submissions, admin). Document the authentication middleware implementation and how it protects different route groups. Explain the serverless API pattern used by Next.js and how endpoints handle requests/responses. Include the authentication flow from login to protected route access. Document error handling strategies, request validation, and response formatting. Address security considerations including CORS, rate limiting, and input sanitization. Explain the integration between middleware, authentication, and API routes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Submission Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Submission model representing booklet creation requests in Titchybook Creator. Document the Submission entity structure including id (String, @id, @default(cuid())), userId (String), user relation, status (String, @default('PENDING')), pdfS3Key (String?), rejectionReason (String?), and timestamps (createdAt, updatedAt). Explain the status workflow with PENDING, APPROVED, and REJECTED states, admin moderation process, and rejection reason handling. Detail the relationship with User model (belongsTo) and SubmissionImage model (hasMany). Include field constraints, default values, and the userId index for performance. Provide examples of submission creation, status updates, and admin approval workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the image processing pipeline that prepares uploaded images for PDF composition. Document the processImageForPanel function that handles image resizing, cropping, and rotation based on panel specifications. Explain the integration with Sharp library for image manipulation, including quality settings, compression strategies, and format conversion to PNG. Detail the parallel processing approach using Promise.all for efficient batch image processing. Document error handling for missing images, invalid formats, and processing failures. Include examples of how images are transformed to fit panel dimensions while maintaining aspect ratios. Address performance optimization techniques and memory management during bulk image processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Validation and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for file validation and security measures in the upload system. Detail the client-side validation rules including supported image formats (JPG, PNG, WebP), size limits (10MB), and MIME type checking. Document the server-side validation implementation in the presigned URL route handler. Explain the security model using AWS S3 presigned URLs to prevent direct server upload bypasses. Cover error handling strategies for invalid files, exceeded size limits, and unsupported formats. Include validation error messages, user feedback mechanisms, and retry logic. Document the integration between client-side and server-side validation to ensure robust security and user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status Monitoring and Visual Indicators
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the status monitoring and visual indicator system. Document the StatusBadge component implementation showing color-coded status representations (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED) with appropriate styling and tooltips. Explain the status value definitions and their corresponding visual treatments. Detail the integration between status values from the database and their display in the UI. Include examples of status transitions throughout the submission lifecycle and how users can interpret status changes. Document accessibility considerations for status indicators and provide guidelines for consistent status messaging across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Submission Moderation Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the submission moderation workflow in Titchybook Creator. Document the complete moderation process from submission review to final approval or rejection. Explain the administrative endpoints for managing submissions including GET requests for listing pending submissions, PATCH/PUT for status updates, and DELETE for removal. Detail the moderation interface components and user interaction patterns. Document the approval/rejection workflow including status transitions, notification systems, and audit trail logging. Include examples of moderation scenarios, bulk operations, and workflow automation. Address moderation queue management, priority handling, and reporting features. Provide guidance on moderation best practices and content policy enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Submission APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for submission management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for user submissions including creation, retrieval, update, and deletion. Explain the page labeling system for booklet layout, image ordering mechanism, and S3 integration for cloud storage. Document the onDelete: Cascade constraint ensuring automatic cleanup when parent submission is deleted. Include field validation rules, indexing strategies, and relationships with Submission model. Provide examples of image upload workflows, ordering systems, and booklet page arrangement patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: PDF Output Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for PDF output management from generation to delivery. Explain the PDFDocument creation process, page setup with proper dimensions, and image embedding with precise positioning. Detail the S3 integration for PDF storage including key generation with buildPdfKey function and upload operations. Document the database update process that stores the pdfS3Key for future retrieval. Explain the API endpoint implementation that triggers PDF generation and handles download requests. Include error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and status tracking throughout the generation process. Address security considerations for PDF access and download management.

wiki_items.title: Download Access and PDF Generation Integration

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Titchybook Creator - Micro Booklet Creation Platform

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Titchybook Creator is a web application designed to transform users' images into printable 8-page micro booklets. The platform enables users to upload images, arrange them in a specific layout, and generate professional-quality PDF booklets ready for printing.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **User-Friendly Image Processing**: Allow users to easily upload and organize images for booklet creation
- **Professional PDF Generation**: Produce high-quality 8-page micro booklets with proper page sequencing
- **Cloud Storage Integration**: Utilize AWS S3 for scalable image and PDF storage
- **Authentication & Authorization**: Implement secure user registration and login system
- **Admin Management**: Provide administrative capabilities for content moderation and submission management

### Target Audience
- **Individual Creators**: Artists, photographers, and hobbyists creating custom booklets
- **Small Businesses**: Local print shops and creative agencies offering custom booklet services
- **Educational Institutions**: Schools and libraries creating educational materials
- **Event Planners**: Professionals creating custom printed materials for special occasions

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js Application] --> B[App Router]
    A --> C[Server Components]
    A --> D[Client Components]
    
    B --> E[Auth Routes]
    B --> F[Protected Routes]
    B --> G[Public Routes]
    B --> H[Admin Routes]
    
    C --> I[Prisma ORM]
    C --> J[AWS S3 Integration]
    C --> K[PDF Generation]
    
    D --> L[React Hooks]
    D --> M[State Management]
    D --> N[UI Components]
    
    I --> O[(SQLite Database)]
    J --> P[S3 Buckets]
    K --> Q[PDF Library]
```

### Design Patterns

The application follows several key architectural patterns:

- **Layered Architecture**: Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers
- **Repository Pattern**: Prisma client abstracts database operations
- **Factory Pattern**: S3 client factory for different storage operations
- **Provider Pattern**: NextAuth SessionProvider for authentication state management
- **Route Handler Pattern**: Next.js App Router with API routes for server-side operations

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Client[Web Client] --> Auth[Authentication Layer]
    Client --> Upload[Image Upload]
    Client --> PDF[PDF Generation]
    
    Auth --> DB[(Database)]
    Upload --> S3[(AWS S3)]
    PDF --> S3
    
    DB --> Prisma[Prisma Client]
    S3 --> S3SDK[AWS SDK]
    
    Prisma --> SQLite[(SQLite)]
    S3SDK --> S3Bucket[S3 Bucket]
```

### Data Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant A as App Router
    participant S as S3 Service
    participant P as Prisma
    participant D as Database
    
    U->>A: Upload Images
    A->>S: Request Presigned URL
    S-->>A: Return Signed URL
    A->>U: Redirect to S3 Upload
    U->>S: Upload to S3
    S->>P: Store Metadata
    P->>D: Insert Records
    D-->>P: Confirm Storage
    P-->>A: Return Submission ID
    A-->>U: Show Upload Success
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Layout**
- `src/app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with providers and global styling
- `src/app/page.tsx` - Landing page component
- `src/middleware.ts` - Authentication and authorization middleware

**Routing Structure**
- `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` - User authentication interface
- `src/app/(auth)/register/page.tsx` - User registration interface
- `src/app/(protected)/create/page.tsx` - Booklet creation interface
- `src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx` - User submission dashboard
- `src/app/(admin)/admin/page.tsx` - Administrative management interface

### Core Modules

**Database Layer**
- `prisma/schema.prisma` - Database schema definition with User, Submission, and SubmissionImage models
- `src/lib/prisma.ts` - Prisma client initialization and configuration
- `prisma/seed.ts` - Database seeding functionality

**Storage Integration**
- `src/lib/s3.ts` - AWS S3 client configuration and utility functions
- `src/app/api/upload/presign/route.ts` - Presigned URL generation for uploads

**Authentication System**
- `src/auth.ts` - NextAuth configuration and callbacks
- `src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts` - Authentication API endpoints

**PDF Generation**
- `src/lib/constants.ts` - Page configuration constants and validation rules
- `src/app/api/submissions/[id]/pdf/route.ts` - PDF generation endpoint

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `.env` files for AWS credentials and database URLs
- `next.config.ts` - Next.js framework configuration
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript compilation settings

**Build System**
- `package.json` - Dependencies and scripts
- `eslint.config.mjs` - Code linting configuration
- `postcss.config.mjs` - CSS processing pipeline

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**
- `next`: 16.1.6 - React-based web application framework
- `react`: 19.2.3 & `react-dom`: 19.2.3 - Frontend rendering library
- `@prisma/client`: 5.22.0 - Database ORM client
- `next-auth`: 5.0.0-beta.30 - Authentication solution

**AWS Integration**
- `@aws-sdk/client-s3`: 3.1009.0 - S3 storage operations
- `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`: 3.1009.0 - Signed URL generation

**Utility Libraries**
- `pdf-lib`: 1.17.1 - PDF manipulation and generation
- `sharp`: 0.34.5 - Image processing and optimization
- `bcryptjs`: 3.0.3 - Password hashing
- `sonner`: 2.0.7 - Notification system

### Integration Points

**Database Integration**
- SQLite database through Prisma ORM
- Automatic schema migrations via Prisma CLI
- Role-based access control (USER/Admin roles)

**Cloud Storage Integration**
- S3 bucket for image and PDF storage
- Presigned URLs for secure uploads/downloads
- Configurable expiration times for security

**Authentication Integration**
- NextAuth with session management
- Protected route middleware
- Role-based authorization system

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Providers]
    A --> C[Header]
    B --> D[Session Provider]
    D --> E[Protected Routes]
    
    E --> F[Create Page]
    E --> G[Dashboard Page]
    
    F --> H[Image Uploader]
    F --> I[Upload Grid]
    
    G --> J[Submission List]
    G --> K[Status Badge]
    
    H --> L[S3 Service]
    I --> L
    J --> M[Prisma Client]
    K --> M
    L --> N[AWS S3]
    M --> O[(SQLite)]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**User Management System**
- Secure user registration and authentication
- Role-based access control (USER/Admin)
- Session management and persistence

**Image Upload & Organization**
- Multi-image upload with drag-and-drop interface
- Image validation (formats: JPEG, PNG, WEBP, size limit: 10MB)
- Page arrangement for 8-page booklet structure
- Real-time preview and validation

**PDF Generation Pipeline**
- Automated PDF creation from uploaded images
- Proper page sequencing and layout optimization
- High-quality PDF export with compression
- Download functionality with signed URLs

**Administrative Features**
- Content moderation dashboard
- Submission status management
- User administration capabilities
- Analytics and reporting

### Implementation Highlights

**Authentication Architecture**
- `src/auth.ts` - Centralized authentication configuration
- `src/middleware.ts` - Route protection and role validation
- `src/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx` - User authentication interface
- `src/components/auth/RegisterForm.tsx` - User registration interface

**Upload & Processing Workflow**
- `src/components/create/ImageUploader.tsx` - File upload component
- `src/components/create/UploadGrid.tsx` - Image arrangement interface
- `src/app/api/upload/presign/route.ts` - Presigned URL generation
- `src/lib/s3.ts` - S3 integration utilities

**Data Management**
- `prisma/schema.prisma` - Entity relationship modeling
- `src/lib/prisma.ts` - Database connection management
- `src/app/api/submissions/route.ts` - Submission CRUD operations

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login/Register
    Authenticated --> UploadImages: Navigate to Create
    Authenticated --> ViewDashboard: Navigate to Dashboard
    UploadImages --> ValidateImages: Submit Images
    ValidateImages --> ProcessImages: Valid Images
    ValidateImages --> UploadImages: Invalid Images
    ProcessImages --> GeneratePDF: Process Complete
    GeneratePDF --> DownloadPDF: PDF Ready
    DownloadPDF --> ViewDashboard: Back to Dashboard
    ViewDashboard --> AdminPanel: Admin Access
    AdminPanel --> ManageSubmissions: Moderate Content
    ManageSubmissions --> ViewDashboard: Return
```

**Performance Considerations**
- Image optimization using Sharp library
- Cloud storage for scalable media handling
- Database indexing for efficient queries
- Session caching for improved response times

**Security Measures**
- Input validation and sanitization
- Role-based access control
- Secure file upload with presigned URLs
- Environment variable configuration for secrets

**Scalability Features**
- Modular component architecture
- Database abstraction layer
- Cloud-native storage solutions
- Stateless authentication design

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [Providers.tsx](src/components/Providers.tsx)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [constants.ts](src/lib/constants.ts)
- [s3.ts](src/lib/s3.ts)

</blog>
